entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan, the crop species of the ICPM1 line

entities.name: ICPM1
entities.description: A male-sterile, indeterminate, semispreading, midseason line of pigeonpea, also known as MS4A or ICPP1

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: The inability of a plant to produce functional pollen, controlled by a single recessive gene

entities.name: ICP1596-2
entities.description: The pigeonpea line from which ICPM1 was derived

entities.name: GROWTH HABIT
entities.description: The indeterminate and semispreading physical growth characteristics of the plant

entities.name: DAYS TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The time taken to reach 50% flowering, which is approximately 127 days for ICPM1

entities.name: SEEDS PER POD
entities.description: The average number of seeds per pod, which is 3.3 for ICPM1

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of 100 seeds, which is 7.3 g for ICPM1

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per hectare, which is 1.27 t/ha for ICPM1

entities.name: MS1
entities.description: A single recessive gene that controls male sterility in the ICPM1 line

entities.name: TRANSLUCENT ANTHERS
entities.description: A physical characteristic used to recognize homozygous male sterile plants 3-4 days before anthesis

entities.name: BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding method used to develop male-sterile forms of various cultivars using ICPM1

entities.name: POLLINATION
entities.description: The process of open or hand pollination used to maintain the ICPM1 line in isolation

entities.name: HYBRID SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing hybrid seeds using ICPM1 as a female parent

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: A trait for which ICPM1 is recommended as a parent to develop resistant male-sterile parents
